target.name: Polar Semiconductor
target.founder: Majority U.S.-owned (2024 recapitalization led by Niobrara Capital and Prysm Capital); formerly owned by Sanken Electric and Allegro MicroSystems

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $700M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Polar Semiconductor is a Growth-stage chip design and semiconductor company that has raised over $700M in total capital, including a 2024 recapitalization led by Niobrara Capital and Prysm Capital. The target operates within a broad Chips & Semiconductors sector universe comprising 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, and Tenstorrent.

Valuation triangulation relies on a single applicable method due to limitations in the peer set. Stage-progression analysis, grounded in the $700M total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement, yielding $840M. A traditional peer-median approach is not applicable; while 10 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy narrower filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) required for robust comparison, and relying on all-sector medians would introduce material bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $714M to $966M, centered on $840M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single method rather than triangulated consensus. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable-company benchmarks specific to Polar's stage and tier within semiconductors, the representativeness of total raised as a valuation floor, and macro-level chip-sector volatility. Investors should recognize this range as preliminary pending access to financial performance metrics or refined peer alignment.
